title: × Elyleymus hirtiflorus
taxonomy: × Elyleymus hirtiflorus is a nothospecies, a hybrid between Elymus and Leymus genera. It belongs to the family Poaceae, commonly known as grasses. Within the family Poaceae, × Elyleymus hirtiflorus is placed in the subfamily Pooideae. The genus Elymus and Leymus are both part of the tribe Triticeae. The classification of × Elyleymus hirtiflorus reflects its unique genetic makeup and characteristics.

intro:
I couldn't find any information on the plant species × Elyleymus hirtiflorus. However, I can provide you with information on the related plant species Elymus hirtiflorus.

Elymus hirtiflorus is a species of grass, specifically a type of wild wheat or a type of bunchgrass. This plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Elymus hirtiflorus include phenolic compounds, flavonoids, and alkaloids. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
